cards.front: What is a balanced search tree?
cards.back: A data structure in which the height of the two subtrees of every node differs by at most one

cards.front: Why are balanced search trees used?
cards.back: To achieve efficient search, insertion, and deletion operations with an average time complexity of O(log n)

cards.front: What is the time complexity of heapify operation?
cards.back: O(n) in the worst case, but can be O(log n) in the best case
A comparison-based sorting algorithm that uses a binary heap data structure to sort elements

cards.front: What is the time complexity of Heap Sort?
cards.back: O(n log n) in all cases (best, average, and worst)
